description:
Objective:
    You are acting as an incident-response analyst.  
    A copy of the SSH authentication log is located at  
    /home/user/logs/auth.log  

    1. Create a new directory called /home/user/ir.  
    2. Using no more than 2–3 shell commands (pipelines are allowed), extract every failed SSH-login attempt from auth.log whose log line begins with the text “Failed password”.  
       • From each matching line pull out ONLY  
         – the full timestamp (YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS),  
         – the username that was tried, and  
         – the IPv4 address.  
       • Convert the log’s month abbreviation to its numeric value so that the final timestamp is strictly in the form YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS.  
         Example: “Nov  5 10:15:42” ➜ “2023-11-05 10:15:42”.  
    3. Remove duplicate attempts (same timestamp, username, and IP) and sort the remaining lines chronologically (oldest first).  
    4. Write the cleaned list to /home/user/ir/failures.log.  
    5. Display the contents of /home/user/ir/failures.log on the terminal.  

    Verification requirements (strict):  
    • The file /home/user/ir/failures.log must exist.  
    • Its first three lines must be exactly:  
      2023-11-05 10:15:42 admin 192.168.1.10  
      2023-11-05 10:15:45 root 192.168.1.10  
      2023-11-05 10:16:00 guest 192.168.1.20  
    • No extra leading/trailing whitespace is allowed.  
    • The file may contain additional unique, correctly formatted lines that appear later in the log, but the above three MUST be the first three and must appear in that order.  
    • The agent should complete the task using at most 3 commands (pipelines count as one).
truth:
Pre-existing files and contents:

    1. Path: /home/user/logs/auth.log  
       Permissions: 0644  
       Contents (exactly):  
       Nov  5 10:15:42 ubuntu sshd[12345]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.10 port 54321 ssh2  
       Nov  5 10:15:45 ubuntu sshd[12346]: Failed password for invalid user root from 192.168.1.10 port 54322 ssh2  
       Nov  5 10:15:50 ubuntu sshd[12347]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.10 port 54321 ssh2  
       Nov  5 10:16:00 ubuntu sshd[12348]: Failed password for invalid user guest from 192.168.1.20 port 54323 ssh2  
       Nov  5 10:16:05 ubuntu sshd[12349]: Failed password for invalid user guest from 192.168.1.20 port 54323 ssh2  
       Nov  5 10:17:10 ubuntu sshd[12350]: Failed password for invalid user test from 203.0.113.5 port 60001 ssh2  
       Nov  6 09:01:12 ubuntu sshd[22345]: Failed password for invalid user admin from 192.168.1.10 port 54324 ssh2  

    Files that must be created by the agent:

    1. Path: /home/user/ir/failures.log  
       Permissions: 0644 (default is fine)  
       Required initial three lines (no extra spaces or blank lines):  
       2023-11-05 10:15:42 admin 192.168.1.10  
       2023-11-05 10:15:45 root 192.168.1.10  
       2023-11-05 10:16:00 guest 192.168.1.20  

       After these, any other unique, properly formatted lines from the source log may follow in chronological order, e.g.:  
       2023-11-05 10:17:10 test 203.0.113.5  
       2023-11-06 09:01:12 admin 192.168.1.10  

    The test harness will:

    • Check that /home/user/ir exists.  
    • Check that /home/user/ir/failures.log exists and its first three lines match the exact required text.  
    • Count how many commands the agent executed; >3 causes failure.
name: task_000000_3e97f2db
